## Overview

**v0.4.1 — cross-platform unification finalized + config schema clean-up.**

This release closes out the cross-platform refactor that started in
v0.4.0. The remaining macOS-side gaps in renderer API parity are gone,
the macOS host module is purged of dead constants and stale legacy
migration code, the `About` dialog is now routed through the shared
wrapper instead of importing the macOS module directly, and the config
schema gets a unit-suffix sweep so every numeric field carries its
unit in the name (`_percent`, `_point`, `_ratio`). Schema-level
breaking changes — see *Migration* below — are not auto-converted; the
old format is rejected with a clear fatal message so users notice.

The Phase-0-through-Phase-7 plan + decisions are tracked in
[CROSS_PLATFORM.md](../../CROSS_PLATFORM.md) and umbrella issue
[#171](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/171).

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.4.1-windows.zip` | unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. Currently unsigned — see *Known limitations* in [README](../../README.md) for AV / SmartScreen handling and elevated auto-start. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.4.1-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: ad-hoc signed, so macOS may block the first
open. Right-click `TildaZ.app` → Open, or run
`xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/TildaZ.app`. Then grant
Accessibility + Input Monitoring permissions once.

## Migration from v0.4.0

The config schema has changed. **Existing users will see a fatal
dialog on first launch with v0.4.1**. Two options:

- **Easy** — delete `tildaz.json` (Windows: `%APPDATA%\tildaz\tildaz.json`,
  macOS: `~/.config/tildaz/tildaz.json`). On the next launch tildaz
  writes a fresh file in the new schema with all defaults.
- **Manual** — edit the file in place. Renames:
  - `window.width` → `window.width_percent` (now a float, e.g. `50.0`)
  - `window.height` → `window.height_percent`
  - `window.offset` → `window.offset_percent`
  - `window.opacity` → `window.opacity_percent`
  - `font.size` → `font.size_point`
  - `font.cell_width` → `font.cell_width_ratio`
  - `font.line_height` → `font.line_height_ratio`
  - `font.family`: was a string *or* array — now a single string (primary).
  - `font.glyph_fallback` (new key): the array of fallback fonts. If your
    old config had `"family": ["X", "Y", "Z"]`, write `"family": "X"`
    and `"glyph_fallback": ["Y", "Z"]`.
  - macOS only: `shell` is now stored as an explicit value (e.g.
    `"/bin/zsh"`). The legacy empty-string sentinel that fell back to
    `$SHELL` env is gone — set the shell explicitly.

Why no auto-migration: silent migration would leave users unaware that
the schema changed, and the rename is small enough that a one-time edit
is worth the clarity. Schema fatals carry the new key name in the
message so the fix is mechanical.

## What's new since v0.4.0

### Cross-platform unification — Phase 0 to 7 (#171)

- **macOS host dead-constant cleanup** (Phase 0). `FONT_FAMILY`,
  `FONT_SIZE_PT`, `CELL_WIDTH_SCALE`, and `LINE_HEIGHT_SCALE` were
  defined in `host/macos.zig` but never read — every call site already
  used `g_config.font_*` directly. The constants and their now-stale
  comment ("hardcoded until config integration") are deleted. Same
  applies to a stale comment block; behavior is unchanged.
- **About dialog wrapper consolidation** (Phase 1). `about.zig` used
  to bypass the platform wrapper and import `dialog/macos.zig`
  directly to call `showAboutAlert`. Now `dialog.zig` exposes
  `showAboutAlert(title, message)` for both platforms (Windows
  forwards to `show(.info, …)` since `MessageBoxW` already handles
  Ctrl+C natively; macOS keeps the `NSTextView`-based accessory view
  for clean Cmd+C routing). Top-level platform-leak gone.
- **Windows-only config helper renaming** (Phase 2).
  `config.fontFamilyUtf16` / `config.shellUtf16` are Windows-only
  (`@compileError` on macOS) but the names read as cross-platform.
  Renamed to `windowsFontFamilyUtf16` / `windowsShellUtf16` so call
  sites flag their platform-specific nature at a glance.
- **macOS shell — first-run `$SHELL` resolution** (Phase 3). The macOS
  `shell` default used to be the empty string `""`, with host code
  branching at runtime to fall back to `$SHELL` env, then `/bin/zsh`.
  Now the host resolves `$SHELL` (or `/bin/bash`) at *first launch*
  only, writes the resolved path explicitly into the new config, and
  thereafter reads the disk value with no fallback. Disk and memory
  always agree; the runtime branch is gone.
- **font.family + font.glyph_fallback schema** (Phase 4). The
  `font.family` array is split into a primary string + a separate
  `glyph_fallback` array. All entries (primary + fallback) must be
  installed; a missing entry trips the chain-dump fatal that already
  existed. Defaults now ship a Korean / emoji / symbol fallback chain
  on both platforms (Windows: Malgun Gothic + Segoe UI Emoji + Segoe
  UI Symbol; macOS: Apple SD Gothic Neo + Apple Color Emoji + Apple
  Symbols), so multi-script terminal output looks right out of the
  box. macOS already had per-entry strict validation
  (`CTFontCopyFamilyName` round-trip in `font/macos/font.zig`); the
  v0.4.1 schema change makes the *intent* of the array explicit
  (fallback chain, not "primary candidates").
- **Renderer API parity** (Phase 5 + 6). The macOS Metal renderer
  exposed a single `renderFrame(layer, terminal, …, tab_titles, …)`
  while Windows already had separate `renderTabBar` + `renderTerminal`.
  Metal now matches: `renderTabBar` begins the frame (acquire drawable
  + command buffer + encoder + clear, stash tab args), `renderTerminal`
  finishes (terminal pass → tabs pass → end encoder → present + commit).
  Frame state lives on the renderer between the two calls — same shape
  as Windows' self.rtv pattern. `renderer.zig` no longer carries an
  `UnsupportedRendererBackend` stub; macOS dispatches through it like
  Windows does.
- **Config schema unit suffixes** (Phase 7, α-전면). Every numeric
  field in the schema now carries its unit in the name, and percent
  values become floats so users can dial fractional values
  (`33.3 %`):
  - `width_percent`, `height_percent`, `offset_percent`,
    `opacity_percent` — all `float`
  - `font.size_point` — `int`
  - `font.cell_width_ratio`, `font.line_height_ratio` — `float`
  - Internal renames mirror the schema:
    `Config.opacity_alpha` (memory u8 alpha; disk is the percent
    float), `window.cell_width_px` / `window.cell_height_px` (the
    pixel-resolved cell size, distinct from the config ratio),
    `cell_width_ratio` / `line_height_ratio` everywhere. The
    cross-file `cell_width` ambiguity (config-ratio vs window-px) is
    resolved.

### Windows — README known-limitation entries

- **Unsigned binary may be blocked by AV / SmartScreen**. New entry
  in README *Known limitations* explains how SmartScreen / EDR may
  block first launch on an unsigned binary, with three workarounds
  (Run anyway, AV allow-list, elevated launch). A copy-pasteable
  PowerShell snippet does both: removes the legacy `HKCU\…\Run`
  entry, and registers a Task Scheduler job at logon with `/RL
  HIGHEST` for elevated auto-start. Pairs with `"auto_start": false`
  in `tildaz.json`. Fully automating this via an
  `auto_start_elevated` config option is tracked as
  [#151](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/151).

### Internal cleanup

- **`autostart/windows.zig` no longer probes Task Scheduler at every
  launch**. The legacy migration path that ran `schtasks /delete /tn
  "TildaZ" /f` on every `enable` / `disable` is gone (along with the
  `STARTUPINFOW`, `PROCESS_INFORMATION`, `CreateProcessW` declarations
  and the `BOOL` type alias that only that code used). This also
  unblocks the *user-installed* admin Task Scheduler entry named
  `TildaZ` documented in *Known limitations* — it would previously
  have been silently deleted on each tildaz launch.
- **Outdated `SF Mono not registered by default` README entry
  removed**. The macOS default font is `Menlo`; SF Mono is only
  reached if a user explicitly names it, so the previous "limitation"
  entry was misleading.
- **Documentation sweep**. `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `SPEC.md`,
  `CONFIG.md`, and `CROSS_PLATFORM.md` are updated to reflect the new
  module file paths (`host/{windows,macos}.zig`,
  `terminal/{windows,macos}/pty.zig`, `renderer/{windows,macos}.zig`,
  `font/{windows,macos}/font.zig`) and the new schema field names.

### Issues created

- [#170](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/170) — config
  hot-reload (disk watch + per-field hot-apply dispatch). New work item.
- [#171](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/171) — umbrella
  tracking the v0.4.1 cross-platform unification phases. Closed
  alongside this release.

## Known issues

- All v0.3.3 known issues still apply — see [`v0.3.3.md`](v0.3.3.md).
- macOS Hanja / kanji *reconversion* (Option+Return on already-committed
  text) is unimplemented
  ([#166](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/166)). Composition-time
  conversion (the part users actually rely on) works normally.

## Verification

- [ ] Both platforms: existing v0.4.0 `tildaz.json` triggers a clear
      fatal dialog naming the changed key. Either deleting the file
      (auto-recreate on next launch) or applying the manual rename
      list above produces a working v0.4.1 config.
- [ ] Both platforms: a multi-script terminal output line — e.g.
      `echo 한글, 日本語, 中文, 😀, ▌` — renders the Korean / Japanese /
      Chinese characters, the emoji, and the box-drawing block all
      correctly out of the box, using the new default
      `glyph_fallback` chain. No "missing glyph" tofu.
- [ ] Both platforms: a fractional percent (`"width_percent": 33.3`)
      docks the window to ~one-third of the screen exactly — not
      33% rounded down to integer math.
- [ ] macOS: first-launch creates a `tildaz.json` whose `shell` field
      contains the user's actual `$SHELL` (e.g. `/bin/zsh`,
      `/usr/local/bin/fish`), not an empty string. Second launch
      reads the disk value with no env fallback.
- [ ] macOS: tab bar interactions from v0.4.0 (rename, drag,
      cursor-follow scroll, IME pre-edit on the renaming tab) all
      still work after the renderer split into `renderTabBar` +
      `renderTerminal`. Single-tab mode hides the tab bar; switching
      from 1 → 2 tabs reveals it without breaking the cell grid.
- [ ] Windows: README's elevated-auto-start PowerShell snippet
      registers a `TildaZ` task at logon with highest privileges, and
      a subsequent regular tildaz launch does *not* delete it (the
      legacy migration is gone).
- [ ] Both platforms: an `font.family` written as an array (old
      schema) → fatal `font.family must be a string`. A
      `font.glyph_fallback` written with non-string elements → fatal
      `font.glyph_fallback must be a list of strings`. A missing
      `font.glyph_fallback` key → standard `missing required key`
      fatal. All three messages name the field cleanly.
